title: Bouchea pumila
intro: Bouchea pumila, also known as the Small Bouchea or Small Bouchea shrub, is a small, shrub-like plant native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can aid in relieving respiratory issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Bouchea pumila include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Bouchea pumila is classified within the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. This classification is based on the plant's botanical characteristics, such as its leaf structure and flower arrangement. As a member of the Lamiaceae family, Bouchea pumila shares similarities with other plants in this family, including their aromatic leaves and clustered flowers. Further classification within the genus Bouchea places Bouchea pumila as a species within this genus. The exact classification may vary depending on the source and the most up-to-date taxonomic information.

Its leaves and stems have been employed to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems.
